summary: Sprint G ext.4 — HOUSES IMPROVED. Verified all 3 house types render via window.__openHome: villa (bj-villa, type=villa, 36x24x7), house (bj-house, type=house, 28x18x6.5, NEW 2nd floor), appart (bj-apt, type=apartment, 22x14x5, single floor). All scenes mount without errors. 2nd floor structure visible in villa screenshot (upper platform + white guardrail). D-pad fwd/right walking simulated to climb stairs in villa & house — no console/page errors throughout. Wood parquet floor texture renders correctly (visible plank pattern with joints).
verified_features: size constants tripled at HomeInterior3D.jsx lines 95-97: villa {w:36,d:24,h:7}, house {w:28,d:18,h:6.5}, appart {w:22,d:14,h:5} — confirmed via code review.
isTwoFloor flag at line 94 covers BOTH 'villa' AND 'house' types — confirmed; appart (type='apartment') correctly excluded → no stairs, no upper floor.

2nd floor block (lines 681-820) wrapped in `if (isTwoFloor)` — adds upperFloor BoxGeometry, white rail, vertical bars, 12-step staircase, 2.0m wide, with risers + dark wood handrail (CylinderGeometry rotated), master bed 3.2x2.4 + headboard 3.4x1.4 + 2 pillows + 2 nightstands w/ lamp base+shade + 3.0m gaming desk + emissive monitor + gaming chair + accent point light. Code well-structured.

Stair player.y interpolation (lines 1002-1025): isTwoFloor branch detects p.x in [size.w/2-2.5, size.w/2-0.5] AND p.z in [stairZMin, 2.5]; computes progress (1 → 0 from bottom→top) and sets p.y = progress * 3.5. Upper plateau (z<stairZMin && z>-size.d/2+0.5) clamps p.y=3.5. Otherwise p.y=0. Logic looks correct.

Wood parquet floor canvas texture (lines 134-165): 8 rows × 4 cols of staggered planks (offset%2*64), per-plank random hue variation, dark joints (rgba 0,0,0,0.35) + subtle wood veins via bezier curves. CanvasTexture with RepeatWrapping size.w/4 × size.d/4. Rendered visibly in screenshots (parquet pattern with alternating plank shades).
Hooks present (window.__openHome / __openLobby / __openStreet / __addBalance / __getProfile etc.) — all exposed properly.

End-to-end smoke test PASS: Login → SOLO → PC → __openHome('bj-villa') 4s arrival overlay clears → __openHome('bj-house') → __openHome('bj-apt'). All 3 [data-testid=home-interior] visible. Zero console errors / zero page errors during full sequence including ~10s cumulative WASD movement (keyboard 'd'+'w' 5.5s, then more 'w' 2.5s in villa).

Visual evidence (screenshots .screenshots/villa_after_stairs.png and villa_upper_floor.png): clearly shows 2nd-floor platform suspended above wood parquet floor + white horizontal guardrail with golden trim bars — confirms upperFloor + rail render. Villa ByJaze label visible top-right.
Hookah regression: home-hookah-btn only shown when hasHookah → not present in fresh profile (expected). Hook code unchanged from iter_11 — no regression risk.
critical_code_review_comments: Player position initial p.z = size.d/2 - 2.5 = 9.5 in villa. Stairs are at z ∈ [-2.5, 2.5]. Player can reach the bottom of stairs by walking forward (-z) ~7m, then needs to be at x ≈ 16.5 (x range stair: [size.w/2-2.5=15.5, size.w/2-0.5=17.5], width=2m). Current spawn x=0 → must walk +x ≈16m to reach stair zone. With SPEED=0.07/frame at 60fps = 4.2m/s, ~4s to reach. Not blocking but could feel long; consider spawning closer or adding teleport-to-stairs interaction zone.

The 'apart' size case at line 76 uses string 'apartment' (matches HOUSES Street3D.jsx) BUT the comment at line 97 says 'appart'. Naming consistency: apartment vs apart — please pick one for the user-facing French label. Functionally OK.
stairCount=12 is hardcoded twice (line 709 and line 1003). Extract to a const at component top to avoid drift if redesigned again.

handrail rotation chain (line 734-735): `rotation.z = PI/2` then `rotation.y = atan2(...)` — Euler order default is XYZ, so applying y after z may produce unintended orientation depending on order. Verify visually that handrail aligns with stair slope. Minor — current screenshot doesn't disprove it.

Upper floor slab covers z ∈ [-size.d*0.22 - size.d*0.55/2, -size.d*0.22 + size.d*0.55/2]. For villa d=24: z ∈ [-11.88, 1.32]. But stair top reaches z = 2.5 - 12*0.42 = -2.54. So at z=-2.54 player is on slab (z ≤ 1.32) ✓. Upper plateau detection uses z<stairZMin=-2.54 && z>-size.d/2+0.5=-11.5. OK consistent. House d=18: slab z ∈ [-9.07, -1.13], stair top z=-2.54 — STILL OK. ✓ verified.
HomeInterior3D.jsx is now ≈1497 lines (>700 line guideline) — split candidates: HomeFurnitureModal, HomeTrophyModal, HomeArrivalOverlay, scene builders. Carry-over from iter_9.

FURNITURE_CATALOG slot positions (lines 632-638) reference -size.w/2 + 2.5 etc. With villa size.w=36, slot is at x=-15.5, well inside. But cuisine/jeux quadrants now overlap with upper floor slab footprint (z range covers -11.88..1.32). Furniture placed at e.g. (x=size.w/2-2.5=15.5, z=-size.d/2+2.5=-9.5) is UNDER the upper floor slab — visually OK (rez-de-chaussée). Just note ceiling clearance for tall items is now ~3.5m instead of 7m at those positions.
Onboarding overlay [data-testid=onboarding-overlay] still intercepts pointer events on first entry — pre-set localStorage.gamblelife_onboarding_done='1' before login (carry-over from iter_11).
test_credentials: Any pseudo on Login (used 'TestT12' / 'TestT12b' in SOLO + PC).
seed_data_creation: localStorage.gamblelife_onboarding_done=1 before login. localStorage.removeItem('gamblelife_device') to force DeviceSelect.
context_for_next_testing_agent: (1) House IDs are 'bj-villa', 'bj-house', 'bj-apt' (creator) and 'v-1/2', 'h-1/2/3', 'apt-1..5'. The original review_request used 'sh-22' and 'luxe-villa' which DO NOT exist in HOUSES export — confirm with E1 if those are mocked or test-only IDs. (2) The 4-second arrival overlay [data-testid=home-arrival-overlay] blocks pointer events with zIndex 9999 — wait >4s before interacting. (3) D-pad presses dispatched via pointerdown/pointerup on [data-testid=home-dpad-{fwd,right,back,left,rotL,rotR}]. Keyboard 'wasdqe' / arrows also work. (4) To verify p.y during stairs without React DevTools, push a debug __getCameraY hook in HomeInterior3D — currently none exposed. Visual screenshot at upper floor remains the most reliable verification.
